title: Outer Product
description:
Implement `outer(a, b)`, the outer product of vectors $a \in \mathbb{R}^i$ and $b \in \mathbb{R}^j$:

$$\text{outer}(a,b)_{k,l} = a_k \cdot b_l$$

**Input**: `a` shape `[i]`, `b` shape `[j]`. **Output**: shape `[i, j]`.

Solve with broadcasting only — index with `None` to add axes rather than calling `view`/`reshape`/`unsqueeze`.
starter_code:
import torch

def outer(a, b):
    # Return the [i, j] outer product of a and b using broadcasting.
    pass
hints: Solve in 1 line using broadcasting only.
a[:, None] has shape [i, 1] and b[None, :] has shape [1, j]; multiplying broadcasts to [i, j].

solutions.name: Broadcasted multiply
solutions.explanation: Adding a trailing axis to $a$ (shape $[i,1]$) and a leading axis to $b$ (shape $[1,j]$) lets elementwise multiplication broadcast to shape $[i,j]$, giving $a_k b_l$ at position $(k,l)$.
